question: What is the admission procedure for Open and Distance Learning (ODL / Distance Education) programmes at ANUCDE? How do I apply for ODL admissions? Admission process at anucde. How to join anucde distance education? Admission procedure. How can I apply?
answer: The admission process for ODL programmes is exclusively online. Applicants should visit https://anucde.info and click on the 'Admission Application form' link. Register with a mobile number and email, fill out the application form, and upload scanned copies of required documents (Photo, Signature, Aadhaar, Caste/PH certificate if applicable, and eligibility documents). The fee must be paid online at the end of the application process. Confirmation of admission is subject to verification of original documents at the ANU CDE office.

question: How do I take admission in the Regular Campus courses of Acharya Nagarjuna University? Regular campus admission process. Non-distance courses.
answer: For regular campus courses (non-distance), please refer to the official university portal at https://nagarjunauniversity.ac.in. ANUCDE only handles Distance Education (ODL) admissions, which are done through https://anucde.info.

question: Can I submit my admission application form manually? Is offline application possible?
answer: No, manual submission of the application form is not permitted. All applications must be submitted online through the official website, https://www.anucde.info. It is recommended to read the prospectus carefully before applying.

question: Is it possible to pay the course fee in installments? Fee payment installments.
answer: No, fee payment in installments is not permitted at ANUCDE. The full year-wise fee must be paid online during the application submission process.

question: Is there any age restriction for studying at ANUCDE? Minimum and maximum age limit.
answer: There is no upper age restriction for taking admission into any programme under the ODL mode at ANUCDE. However, for Undergraduate programmes, applicants must have attained 18 years of age by specific dates (June 30 for the July session, or December 31 for the January session).

question: What are the different academic sessions (batches) offered by ANUCDE? Calendar year and Academic year sessions.
answer: As per UGC-DEB guidelines, ANU CDE offers two batches every academic year: a) Academic session beginning January for the Calendar Year, and b) Academic session beginning July for the Academic Year. The Admission Notification for CALENDAR YEAR-2026 is currently available on the official website.

question: How many programmes does ANUCDE offer? What are the courses I can take? List of available courses.
answer: ANU CDE offers 14 Post Graduate programmes, 13 Under Graduate programmes, 2 Diploma programmes, and 2 Certificate programmes. Detailed information is available on the official website.

question: What is the medium of instruction for the programmes? English or Telugu medium.
answer: English is the medium of instruction for most programmes. Some programmes are also offered in regional languages such as Telugu. Please refer to the specific admission notification and programme structure for details.

question: Is the admission fee refundable if I cancel my admission? Refund policy.
answer: No, according to ANU CDE norms, the fee once paid is non-refundable and non-adjustable under any circumstances. However, students may be allowed to change to another programme if they meet the eligibility requirements.

question: How long does the admission confirmation process take? When will I get my seat confirmed? Admission approval timeline.
answer: Admission confirmation typically takes a maximum of 2 months from the date of application, following the verification of original documents.

question: What are the eligibility criteria and required documents for Undergraduate (UG) programmes? UG admission requirements.
answer: A pass in 10+2 or equivalent is required. For documents, age proof is mandatory (SSC/10th certificate or Birth Certificate prioritize). Original documents should be scanned and uploaded during the online application process.

question: What are the eligibility criteria and required documents for Postgraduate (PG) programmes? PG admission requirements.
answer: Applicants must have a Graduation Original Degree or Provisional Certificate and a Memorandum of Marks from a qualifying examination. A pass in the ANUCDE Entrance Test is specifically required for MBA and MCA courses. Age proof is also mandatory.

question: What is the procedure and fee for changing a programme? Course change process.
answer: Programme changes are generally not permitted. However, if a student is ineligible or desires a change, they must apply via the student login within 15 days of provisional admission. A fee of Rs. 500/- is applicable.

question: What document is used as the basis for the name on student certificates? Correction in name.
answer: The student's name on certificates is based on the SSC (10th Class) certificate or, in its absence, a Birth Certificate issued by a competent authority. Married female candidates wishing to use a changed surname must submit the original Gazette Notification of the name change.

question: How can I access the programme syllabus and course content? Download syllabus.
answer: The syllabus and content for all programmes are available for download on the official website, https://anucde.info, under the 'Programmes' or 'Study Material' sections.

question: Does ANUCDE provide guidance or classes for the programmes? Personal Contact Programme (PCP) classes.
answer: Yes, students are allotted to Learner Support Centres (LSCs) where Personal Contact Programme (PCP) classes are conducted, typically on Saturdays and Sundays. These classes provide guidance through empanelled Academic Counsellors.

question: When are the examinations conducted at ANUCDE? Exam schedule.
answer: Examinations are conducted twice a year: once in June and once in December.

question: What is the examination evaluation pattern? Internal marks and assignments.
answer: The evaluation consists of two parts: continuous assessment through assignments (30% weightage) and a summative assessment through end-semester examinations (70% weightage).

question: Do I need to pay a separate examination fee? Exam fees and dues.
answer: Regular examination fees are generally included in the admission fee. However, students appearing for supplementary examinations or those with backlog papers must pay a prescribed fee as per the examination notification.

question: How and where are the examinations conducted? Examination centers.
answer: Examinations are conducted in pen-and-paper mode at verified and approved examination centres. The details of the assigned centre are printed on the hall ticket, which can be downloaded from the website.

question: How can I download my examination hall ticket? Hall tickets availability. Where to get hall tickets?
answer: Examination hall tickets can be downloaded from the official website https://anucde.info. For example, the January 2026 Examination Halltickets (Semester 1, 2, and 4) are currently available online. The specific name of the examination centre and place will be printed on the hall ticket.

question: When are examination results declared? Check results online. How to check results? Step by step guide to see results.
answer:
Results are typically declared within two months after examinations on the official website https://www.anucde.info. 

**To check your results:**
1. Visit the results portal at https://anucde.info/Results.php
2. Enter your **Hall Ticket Number** (Enrollment Number).
3. Enter the **Captcha** code shown on the screen.
4. Click on 'Submit' to view your marksheet. 

[Note: There is NO username/password login required for checking results.]

question: How do I obtain my certificates after completing a programme? Degree certificate collection.
answer: Certificates are sent by post to the address provided in the application, provided there are no fee dues and document verification is complete. It is important to update any change in residential address via the student login.

question: What are the latest notifications and announcements? News and events.
answer: Latest updates include: 1. Admission Notification for CALENDAR YEAR-2026 is published. 2. January 2026 Examination Halltickets are available. 3. 6th Semester Results for December 2025 are declared. 4. Assignment Question Papers for December 2025 are available online on https://www.anucde.info. Visit our website for more details.
